description: Per-service operational schema: ticket ID prefixes, alias prefixes, and RBAC ownership configuration. Kept separate from service-registry.json (agent routing) and role-permission-registry.json (role definitions). Adding a new service = add one entry here. No code changes required.
_uc05_doc: Top-level UC-5 Triage policy blocks (locked 2026-05-29 per ai-service usecase.md spec). Both blocks are consumed by oneops.use_cases.uc05_triage during proposal assembly and apply.py.

sla_duration_by_priority._doc: Maps the Motadata priority (output of the impact-urgency matrix) to the SLA window duration. apply.py reads this on operator Yes click to set sla_due = now() + duration. Values use a compact form parsed by oneops: '8h', '4h', '2h', '30min'. Editable per tenant without code change.

triage_confidence_tiers._doc: Three-tier routing policy per ai-service usecase.md Step 1 confidence rule. overall_confidence_score >= auto_apply_at -> tier 'auto' (would auto-apply; UC-5 still gates via binary Yes/No for safety). >= propose_at -> tier 'propose' (default proposal card). below refuse_below -> tier 'refuse' (TriageRefusal, no proposal made).

services._priority_doc: Motadata ServiceOps Priority Matrix — verbatim from Admin -> Request Management -> Priority Matrix. UC-5's prioritize_entity reads this; the LLM never decides priority alone (rule C10 deterministic + 2.13 agents-as-data). When Motadata's auto-priority feature is enabled, this is the authoritative source.

priority_matrix._priority_aliases_doc: Read-side alias from legacy ServiceNow-style P-prefix vocabulary to the Motadata 4-axis canonical. Used by oneops.use_cases.uc05_triage to normalise any priority value read from existing rows before display or comparison. WRITE side always uses the Motadata canonical (Low/Medium/High/Urgent) — UC-5 never persists a P-prefix value. Unknown aliases fall through to the input value unchanged with basis 'unrecognised_alias' captured in audit.

services._retrieval_doc: Schema-driven retrieval config consumed by UC-5 (and future UC-2). The retrieval engine builds the cosine/FTS SQL from this block — adding/removing a column = JSON edit, not a code change. neighbour_columns lists the columns fetched onto every neighbour row; status_filter and age_filter_days are hard pre-filters (same-tenant + same-service is implied); aggregation_targets are the columns Tool 1 derives majority suggestions for.

retrieval_schema._change_note: 2026-05-29 PM: aggregation_targets realigned to ai-service usecase.md UC-5 spec. service_name dropped (not in spec). assigned_to + ci_id added (Step 4 agent + Step 5 CI auto-link). Embedding text builder still uses service_name and CI fields as retrieval signal — that is unchanged. 2026-05-30: added embedding_table_v2 + embedding_chunk_type for P3 substrate swap. Reader selects between legacy inline column and new substrate via UC05_EMBEDDING_SOURCE env flag.

services._priority_doc: Requests reuse the Motadata matrix from incident; impact and urgency are derived per the catalog item and SLA pressure (pattern (a) locked with operator 2026-05-29). UC-5's prioritize_entity for requests reads derive_impact_for_request and derive_urgency_for_request, then looks up the incident.priority_matrix.

derive_impact_for_request._doc: Catalog category -> impact mapping. UC-5 reads this in prioritize_entity for requests.

derive_urgency_for_request._doc: SLA pressure -> urgency mapping. UC-5 reads this in prioritize_entity for requests. SLA pressure is computed as fraction of SLA window remaining.

services._retrieval_doc: Same schema-driven contract as incident — different columns reflect the request shape (no subcategory/service_name; has catalog_item_id). Status filter includes 'approved' which is request-specific. UC-2 will read this same block with a lower threshold.

retrieval_schema._change_note: 2026-05-29 PM: aggregation_targets realigned to spec. catalog_item_id dropped (not in spec). assigned_to + ci_id added. Embedding text builder still uses catalog_name + catalog_category as retrieval signal — that is unchanged. 2026-05-30: added embedding_table_v2 + embedding_chunk_type for P3 substrate swap.
